calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions, Specifically For Order: 1) Approving Settlement Agreement; And 2) Reserving Jurisdiction To Enforce Settlement Agreement Under Ccp 664.6; And 3) For Such Other Relief As The Probate Court Deems Just And Proper Pursuant To Its Equitable Powers
Off Calendar Jun-03-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alicia Gamez for and with Louise Stephens in person.
Emille Calhoun for Anita Asturias and Frank Asturias in person.

Discussion was held regarding the settlement agreement, that it is in the best of all beneficairs of the trust, and that parties conducted a meet and confer in the hallway of the courts to make sure all terms and conditions are agreeable to the proposed order.

The matter is taken under submission. Petition for Instructions etc., is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

Petition For 1) Instructions To Co-Trustees 2) To Enjoin Rental Of Trust Property Pending Further Orders Of The Court; 3) For A Temporary Restraining
Off Calendar Jun-03-2026 Off Calendar

Petition For 1) Instructions To Co-Trustees 2) To Enjoin Rental Of Trust Property Pending Further Orders Of The Court; 3) For A Temporary Restraining
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For 1) Instructions To Co-Trustees 2) To Enjoin Rental Of Trust Property Pending Further Orders Of The Court; 3) For A Temporary Restraining
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Continued To Mar-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For 1) Instructions To Co-Trustees 2) To Enjoin Rental Of Trust Property Pending Further Orders Of The Court; 3) For A Temporary Restraining
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Continued To Jan-28-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared in person for and with Louisa Stephens (petitioner).
Emilie Calhoun appeared in person for and with Anita and Frank Austurias (respondents).

Discussion was held regarding the court's August 19th ex parte order, that it was a temporary restraining order rather than a preliminary injunction, that Mr. O'Sullivan is considering seeking a preliminary injunction and post a bond, and that mediation has been encouraged by the Courts.

Ms. Calhoun stated that her client has been doing her best to work with the co-trustee, that majority of the parties agree to rent the properties, and that the proceedings should be suspended.

The Court shall extend the temporary restraining order until the next hearing date.

The matter is continued to January 28, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.
